taskDescription: Click on the key embryological terms in the text below.
textField:
The *blastocyst* begins *implantation* around Day 6-7 on the *posterior* wall of the uterine *fundus*. The outer *trophoblast* differentiates into the inner *cytotrophoblast* (mitotically active cellular layer) and the outer *syncytiotrophoblast* (multinucleated invasive layer that erodes into the *endometrium*). The *syncytiotrophoblast* produces *hCG* (human *chorionic* gonadotropin), which maintains the *corpus* *luteum* during early pregnancy.

The inner cell mass (*embryoblast*) forms the *bilaminar* embryonic disc with two layers: the *epiblast* (dorsal, columnar cells) and the *hypoblast* (ventral, cuboidal cells). Two cavities develop: the *amniotic* cavity dorsal to the epiblast and the primary *yolk* sac ventral to the hypoblast. The *extraembryonic* *mesoderm* forms between the trophoblast and yolk sac, eventually splitting to create the *chorionic* cavity.

The *implantation* window corresponds to Days 20-24 of the menstrual cycle when the endometrium is maximally *receptive*. *Ectopic* pregnancy most commonly occurs in the *ampulla* of the uterine tube. The second week is sometimes called the week of *twos* because of the formation of two cavities, two germ layers, and two trophoblast layers.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key embryological terms accurately.
